title: Haplophyllum patavinum

The plant's classification as a member of the Rutaceae family and the genus Haplophyllum reflects its botanical relationships with other plants in these groups. intro: Haplophyllum patavinum, also known as Patavinian spurge or Patavinian barberry, is a type of plant in the Rutaceae family, characterized by its shrub-like habit and small, yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to treat various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Haplophyllum patavinum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
